body.p: The following operations are all under PostgreSQL's shell panel.

section.title: Mapping SequoiaDB to PostgreSQL
section.p: 2) Set the parameters to connect SequoiaDB
section.codeblock: foo=# create server sdb_server foreign data wrapper sdb_fdw options(address '127.0.0.1', service '11810', user 'sdbadmin', password 'mypassword');
section.note: If there is no password verification, you can ignore field user and password.
section.p: 3) Mapping SequoiaDB' scollection space and collection
section.codeblock: foo=# create foreign table test (name text, id numeric) server sdb_server options ( collectionspace 'chen', collection 'test' ) ;

note.p: The collection space and collection must exist in SequoiaDB database created.
By default, tables of PG are mapped to SequoiaDB as lower case letters, if you want to set it as capital, please see the Notice later in this page.
The mapping of array type in SequoiaDB will also be shown in Notice later.

section.codeblock: foo=# update test set id=9 where name='one';

section.title: Things to Pay Attention When Using
section.p: Collection Space, Collection and Field name in SequoiaDB are case sensitive.
For example, in SequoiaDB, there is a collection space named TEST, and a collection named CHEN, the mapping type in PostgreSQL will be:
section.codeblock: foo=# create foreign table sdb_upcase_cs_cl (name text) server sdb_server options ( collectionspace 'TEST', collection 'CHEN' ) ;
section.p: For example, in SequoiaDB, there is a collection space named foo, and a collection named bar, and they contain data of:
Mapping type in PostgreSQL will be :
section.codeblock: foo=# create foreign table sdb_upcase_field ("NAME" text) server sdb_server options ( collectionspace 'foo', collection 'bar' ) ;
section.p: 2.2) Mapping the Data Type in SequoiaDB
For example, in SequoiaDB, there is a collection space named foo, and a collection named bar, and they contain data of:
The mapping table in PostgreSQL will be:
section.codeblock: foo=# create foreign table bartest (name numeric[], id numeric) server sdb_server options ( collectionspace 'foo', collection 'bar' ) ;
section.p: When SequoiaDB's coord node that PostgreSQL connecting re-starts, and the error is
section.codeblock: ERROR: Unable to get collection "chen.test", rc = -15 HINT: Make sure the collectionspace and collection exist on the remote database

section.title: Edit the Configuration File of PostgreSQL
section.p: 1) View the default configuration in pg_shell:
section.codeblock: foo=#\set AUTOCOMMIT = 'on' PROMPT1 = '%/%R%# ' PROMPT2 = '%/%R%# ' PROMPT3 = '>> ' VERBOSITY = 'default' VERSION = 'PostgreSQL 9.3.4 on x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, compiled by gcc (SUSE Linux) 4.3.4 [gcc-4_3-branch revision 152973], 64-bit' DBNAME = 'foo' USER = 'sdbadmin' PORT = '5432' ENCODING = 'UTF8'
section.p: 2) Change the number of record to get each time doing pg_shell:

note.p: Change to getting 100 records each time doing the pg_shell.
Edit the configuration file in pg_shell directly, and the configuration will only activate in current pg_shell. You need to do the configuration again if you re-start the pg_shell.

section.p: 3) Change the configuration file, get the number of records getting each time.

note.p: 如果显示目录不存在，自己手动创建即可。

section.p: 将需要修改的参数写入配置文件中：
4) Change the logs' level of pg_shell:
5) Change the logs' level of pg engine:
